description: Nutrient-dense sprouts grown from alfalfa seeds, commonly used in salads and sandwiches.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - K, C, B9 (Folate), B1 (Thiamin), B2 (Riboflavin), B5 (Pantothenic Acid). Minerals - Manganese, Copper, Iron, Magnesium, Phosphorus, Zinc.
how_to_grow: Grow indoors any time of year. Soak seeds overnight, spread in shallow tray or jar with mesh lid. Rinse 2-3 times daily. Keep in indirect light. Sprouts ready in 3-7 days.
insect_control: Minimal pest issues indoors. Ensure good air circulation to prevent mold.
tips: Use organic seeds. Refrigerate harvested sprouts within a week. Rotate batches for continuous supply.

description: Deciduous tree producing nutrient-rich nuts requiring warm dry summers and mild winters.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - E, B2 (Riboflavin), B3 (Niacin). Minerals - Manganese, Magnesium, Copper, Phosphorus, Calcium, Iron, Zinc, Potassium.
how_to_grow: Plant in full sun in well-drained deep loamy soil. Trees need cross-pollination. Space 15-20 feet apart. Water deeply but infrequently. Prune annually.
insect_control: Watch for navel orangeworm, peach twig borer, mites. Use dormant oil sprays in winter.
tips: Harvest when hulls split. Shake trees to drop nuts. Dry in sun 1-2 days. Trees bear at 3-4 years.

description: Succulent plant known for medicinal gel used to treat burns and skin conditions.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - A, C, E, B9 (Folate), B12. Minerals - Calcium, Magnesium, Zinc, Chromium, Selenium, Iron, Potassium, Copper, Manganese.
how_to_grow: Grow in well-drained sandy soil in full sun to partial shade. Water deeply but infrequently. Excellent container plant. Propagate from offsets.
insect_control: Few pest problems. Watch for mealybugs and scale. Avoid overwatering causing root rot.
tips: Harvest outer leaves first. Gel most potent from mature leaves (3+ years).

description: Deciduous fruit tree with thousands of varieties suited to different climates.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - C, K, B6 (Pyridoxine), B2 (Riboflavin), A. Minerals - Potassium, Manganese, Copper, Magnesium, Phosphorus.
how_to_grow: Plant in full sun with well-drained slightly acidic soil. Most need cross-pollination. Space standard 25-30ft, dwarf 8-10ft. Prune annually late winter.
insect_control: Codling moth, apple maggot, aphids. Use pheromone traps, kaolin clay, dormant oil.
tips: Choose varieties for your chill hours. Dwarf rootstocks for small gardens. Harvest when fruit separates with slight twist.

description: Stone fruit tree producing sweet orange-fleshed fruits. Blooms early, susceptible to late frosts.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - A, C, E, K, B3 (Niacin). Minerals - Potassium, Copper, Manganese, Phosphorus, Iron.
how_to_grow: Plant in full sun in well-drained soil. Needs winter chill. Space 15-20ft. Prune to open center. Thin fruit 2-3 inches apart.
insect_control: Brown rot, bacterial canker, peach twig borer. Copper spray in fall. Dormant oil in winter.
tips: Harvest when fully colored and slightly soft. Excellent for drying and preserves. Self-fertile varieties available.

description: Peppery salad green that grows quickly in cool weather. Also known as rocket.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - K, A, C, B9 (Folate). Minerals - Calcium, Potassium, Magnesium, Manganese, Iron, Phosphorus.
how_to_grow: Direct sow in early spring or fall. Bolts in heat. Partial shade in summer. Succession sow every 2-3 weeks.
insect_control: Flea beetles main pest. Use row covers. Neem oil for heavy infestations.
tips: Baby leaves milder, mature more peppery. Self-seeds readily. Grows well in containers.

description: Perennial vegetable producing spears each spring for 15-20 years once established.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - K, B9 (Folate), C, A, B1 (Thiamin), B2 (Riboflavin), B6 (Pyridoxine), B3 (Niacin). Minerals - Manganese, Copper, Phosphorus, Potassium, Iron, Zinc, Magnesium, Selenium, Calcium.
how_to_grow: Full sunshine and exceptional drainage. Amend soil with compost. pH above 6. Dig trench 6in deep. Plant crowns 1ft apart. Full harvest 2 years from crown.
insect_control: Asparagus rust, slugs, asparagus beetles. Hand remove beetles. Beer traps for slugs. Buy rust-resistant strains.
tips: Start new bed every 10 years. Avoid crown rot by ensuring good drainage. Raised beds help.

description: Tropical evergreen tree producing creamy nutrient-dense fruits high in healthy fats.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - K, B9 (Folate), C, B5 (Pantothenic Acid), B6 (Pyridoxine), E. Minerals - Potassium, Copper, Magnesium, Manganese, Phosphorus, Zinc, Iron.
how_to_grow: Full sun with excellent drainage. Frost sensitive. Space 20-30ft. Mulch heavily away from trunk. Water deeply and regularly.
insect_control: Avocado thrips, persea mites, Phytophthora root rot. Good drainage essential. Horticultural oil for mites.
tips: Plant type A and B varieties for pollination. Fruit ripens off tree. Trees from seed take 5-13 years to fruit.

description: Tropical herbaceous plant producing clusters of sweet fruit. Grows rapidly in warm humid conditions.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - B6 (Pyridoxine), C, B2 (Riboflavin), B9 (Folate), B3 (Niacin). Minerals - Potassium, Manganese, Magnesium, Copper, Phosphorus.
how_to_grow: Full sun in rich well-drained soil. Needs warmth above 60F and humidity. Space 8-10ft. Water heavily, mulch deeply. Feed monthly.
insect_control: Banana weevil, nematodes, Panama disease. Use clean planting material. Trap weevils with pseudostem pieces.
tips: Fruit matures 9-12 months after flowering. Cut bunch when first fruits yellow. Cut stalk to ground after fruiting.

description: Aromatic annual herb essential in Mediterranean and Asian cuisines with antibacterial volatile oils.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - K, A, C. Minerals - Iron, Calcium, Manganese, Magnesium, Potassium.
how_to_grow: Annual where frost common. Sunny protected area with healthy soil. Sow indoors early spring. Transplant 1ft apart after last frost. Keep moist. Remove flower buds.
insect_control: Minor pest problems. Hand pick Japanese beetles.
tips: Save seeds by harvesting stems after ripening. Hang upside down to catch seeds.

description: Versatile legume in bush and pole varieties. Fixes nitrogen in soil improving fertility.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - K, C, B9 (Folate), B1 (Thiamin), B6 (Pyridoxine). Minerals - Manganese, Iron, Phosphorus, Magnesium, Potassium, Copper, Zinc.
how_to_grow: Direct sow after last frost when soil warm (60F+). Bush beans 3-4in apart, pole beans 6in with support. Full sun. Water regularly, avoid wetting foliage.
insect_control: Mexican bean beetles, aphids, bean rust. Row covers early. Hand pick beetles. Neem oil for aphids.
tips: Do not soak seeds before planting. Inoculate with rhizobium. Succession plant every 2-3 weeks.

description: Root vegetable with edible leaves and sweet earthy roots rich in betalains.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - B9 (Folate), C, B5 (Pantothenic Acid), B6 (Pyridoxine). Minerals - Manganese, Potassium, Iron, Magnesium, Copper, Phosphorus.
how_to_grow: Direct sow 2-4 weeks before last frost. Thin to 3-4in apart. Full sun to partial shade. Keep moist. Harvest roots at 1.5-3in diameter.
insect_control: Leaf miners main pest. Remove affected leaves. Row covers prevent egg laying. Neem oil for flea beetles.
tips: Soak seeds overnight for faster germination. Both roots and greens edible. Store roots in cool sand for months.

description: Vigorous bramble producing sweet-tart berries. Thornless varieties available.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - C, K, A, E, B9 (Folate). Minerals - Manganese, Copper, Potassium, Magnesium, Iron, Zinc.
how_to_grow: Full sun in well-drained slightly acidic soil. Space 3-5ft in rows 8ft apart. Trellis support. Prune spent canes after fruiting. Mulch heavily.
insect_control: Spotted wing drosophila, spider mites, cane borers. Bird netting. Prune infected canes. Dormant spray.
tips: Harvest when fully black and pull easily. Does not ripen after picking. Freeze on trays before bagging.

description: Acid-loving shrub producing antioxidant-rich berries. Requires very acidic soil.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - C, K, E, B6 (Pyridoxine). Minerals - Manganese, Copper, Potassium, Iron.
how_to_grow: Full sun in very acidic soil (pH 4.5-5.5). Amend with peat moss and sulfur. Space 4-6ft. Mulch with pine needles. Plant 2+ varieties for cross-pollination.
insect_control: Bird netting essential. Blueberry maggot and mummy berry. Remove infected berries.
tips: Use acidifying fertilizer in spring. Berries ripen over weeks; harvest every few days. Bushes produce 20+ years.

description: Asian green with crisp white stalks and dark green leaves. Fast-growing cool-season crop.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - A, C, K, B6 (Pyridoxine), B9 (Folate). Minerals - Calcium, Potassium, Manganese, Iron, Magnesium, Phosphorus.
how_to_grow: Direct sow in early spring or fall. Bolts in heat. Space 6-12in. Keep moist. Harvest whole plant or outer leaves. Matures 45-60 days.
insect_control: Flea beetles, aphids, cabbage worms. Row covers. Bt for caterpillars. Neem oil.
tips: Baby bok choy matures faster (30 days). Succession plant. Excellent in stir-fries. Tolerates light frost.

description: Cool-season cruciferous vegetable with dense clusters of green flower buds. High in sulforaphane.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - C, K, A, B9 (Folate), B6 (Pyridoxine), B2 (Riboflavin), E. Minerals - Chromium, Manganese, Phosphorus, Potassium, Copper, Magnesium, Iron, Calcium.
how_to_grow: Start indoors 6-8 weeks before last frost. Full sun, rich well-drained soil. Space 18-24in. Keep moist. Harvest central head when buds tight. Side shoots continue.
insect_control: Cabbage worms, aphids, flea beetles. Row covers. Bt for caterpillars. Companion plant with herbs.
tips: Harvest in morning when heads tight. Side shoots extend harvest for weeks. Blanch and freeze for storage.

description: Cool-season crop producing small cabbage-like heads along a tall stalk. Flavor improves after frost.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - K, C, A, B9 (Folate), B6 (Pyridoxine), B1 (Thiamin). Minerals - Manganese, Iron, Potassium, Phosphorus, Magnesium, Copper.
how_to_grow: Start indoors in spring for fall harvest. Full sun, rich soil. Space 24-30in. Stake tall plants. Harvest from bottom up when 1-2in diameter.
insect_control: Cabbage worms, aphids. Bt spray, row covers, companion planting. Remove yellowing leaves.
tips: Flavor sweetens after frost. Top plant 3-4 weeks before harvest. Can overwinter in mild climates.

description: Deep-rooted biennial with edible roots used in Asian cuisine and traditional medicine.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - B6 (Pyridoxine), B9 (Folate), C, E. Minerals - Manganese, Magnesium, Iron, Potassium, Phosphorus, Calcium, Copper, Zinc.
how_to_grow: Direct sow in spring in deeply worked loose soil. Roots grow 2-3ft deep. Space 6in. Keep moist. Harvest roots in fall of first year.
insect_control: Few pest problems. Occasionally aphids. Strong plant outcompetes weeds.
tips: Loosen soil deeply for straight roots. Young leaves and stalks also edible. Popular in Japanese cuisine as gobo.

description: Cool-season crop forming dense heads. Versatile from coleslaw to sauerkraut.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - K, C, B6 (Pyridoxine), B9 (Folate), B1 (Thiamin), B5 (Pantothenic Acid). Minerals - Manganese, Calcium, Potassium, Magnesium, Iron, Phosphorus.
how_to_grow: Start indoors 6-8 weeks before last frost. Full sun, rich moist soil. Space 12-24in. Keep consistently watered. Harvest when heads firm.
insect_control: Cabbage loopers, imported cabbage worms, aphids, root maggots. Bt, row covers, stem collars.
tips: Split heads from uneven watering. Store whole heads in root cellar for months. Red varieties more antioxidants.

description: Drought-tolerant succulent with edible pads (nopales) and sweet fruits (tunas).
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - C, B6 (Pyridoxine), B2 (Riboflavin), A. Minerals - Magnesium, Calcium, Potassium, Manganese, Iron, Copper.
how_to_grow: Plant pads in sandy well-drained soil in full sun. Let cut end callus a week before planting. Minimal water once established. Space 3-5ft.
insect_control: Cochineal scale (white cottony masses). Remove with water spray or alcohol. Cactus moth larvae.
tips: Harvest young pads at 4-8in for eating. Use tongs, scrape off spines. Fruits ripen to red/purple. Wear thick gloves.

description: Root vegetable rich in beta-carotene. Many varieties from short to long in various colors.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - A (beta-carotene), K, C, B6, B1, B3. Minerals - Potassium, Manganese, Phosphorus, Magnesium.
how_to_grow: Direct sow in loose stone-free soil. Mix tiny seeds with sand. Thin to 2-3in. Keep moist until germination (10-21 days). Full sun.
insect_control: Carrot rust fly main pest. Row covers. Companion plant with onions. Avoid thinning in evening.
tips: Loose sandy soil for straight roots. Heavy soil causes forking. Sweeten after light frost. Store in sand.

description: Cool-season crop producing dense white heads. More temperamental than other brassicas.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - C, K, B6, B9 (Folate), B5. Minerals - Manganese, Potassium, Magnesium, Phosphorus, Iron.
how_to_grow: Start indoors 6-8 weeks before last frost. Rich moist soil, full sun. Space 18-24in. Blanch white varieties by tying leaves over head. Harvest when compact.
insect_control: Cabbage worms, aphids, flea beetles. Bt, row covers, neem oil. Good air circulation.
tips: Temperature fluctuations cause buttoning. Colored varieties dont need blanching. Harvest before curds separate.

description: Crunchy stalk vegetable requiring consistent moisture and long cool growing season.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - K, C, A, B9 (Folate), B2, B6. Minerals - Potassium, Manganese, Calcium, Magnesium, Phosphorus.
how_to_grow: Start indoors 10-12 weeks before last frost. Rich moist soil. Space 8-10in. Consistent moisture. Blanch stalks by mounding soil. Long season (120-180 days).
insect_control: Aphids, celery leaf tier, slugs. Insecticidal soap. Hand pick caterpillars. Beer traps.
tips: Cutting celery easier to grow than stalk celery. Mulch heavily. Bolts in heat.

description: Aromatic herb with daisy-like flowers used for calming tea.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - A, B9 (Folate). Minerals - Calcium, Magnesium, Potassium, Manganese, Iron, Zinc, Copper.
how_to_grow: Direct sow spring or fall. Seeds need light; press into surface. Thin to 6-8in. Full sun to partial shade. Drought tolerant. Self-seeds.
insect_control: Few pest problems. Strong scent deters many insects. Good companion plant.
tips: Harvest flowers when petals fold back. Dry on screens. German chamomile is annual; Roman is perennial.

description: Deciduous fruit trees producing sweet or tart cherries.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - C, A, K, B6. Minerals - Potassium, Copper, Manganese, Magnesium, Iron, Phosphorus.
how_to_grow: Full sun, well-drained soil. Sweet need cross-pollination; tart self-fertile. Space 20-25ft standard, 8-12ft dwarf. Prune to open center. Net for birds.
insect_control: Cherry fruit fly, black cherry aphid, brown rot. Sticky traps, dormant oil, copper spray.
tips: Harvest when fully colored. Sweet cherries bruise easily. Tart release from stem when ripe. Dwarf varieties for small spaces.

description: Perennial herb with mild onion flavor. Purple flowers also edible.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - K, C, A, B9 (Folate). Minerals - Calcium, Iron, Magnesium, Manganese, Potassium.
how_to_grow: Start from seed or divisions in spring. Full sun to partial shade. Space 6-8in. Cut back regularly. Divide every 3-4 years. Very cold hardy.
insect_control: Virtually pest-free. Repels many garden pests. Good companion for roses and tomatoes.
tips: Freeze chopped in ice cube trays. Flowers attract pollinators. Garlic chives have flat leaves and white flowers.

description: Dual-purpose herb: leaves (cilantro) fresh, seeds (coriander) as spice. Bolts quickly in heat.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - K, A, C, B9 (Folate). Minerals - Potassium, Manganese, Iron, Magnesium, Calcium, Copper.
how_to_grow: Direct sow in cool weather. Bolts in heat. Succession sow every 2-3 weeks. Partial shade in summer. Space 2-3in. Keep moist.
insect_control: Few pest problems. Strong scent deters insects. Good companion plant.
tips: Slow-bolt varieties extend harvest. Let some flower for coriander seeds. Self-seeds readily. Roots edible in Thai cuisine.

description: Heat-tolerant brassica with large nutritious leaves. Staple of Southern US cuisine.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - K, A, C, B9 (Folate), B6, E, B2. Minerals - Manganese, Calcium, Iron, Magnesium, Phosphorus, Potassium, Copper.
how_to_grow: Direct sow or transplant spring or late summer. Full sun. Space 18-24in. Rich moist soil. Harvest outer leaves. Tolerates heat and cold.
insect_control: Cabbage worms, aphids, flea beetles. Bt, row covers, neem oil. Harlequin bugs in warm weather.
tips: Flavor improves after frost. Harvest all winter in mild climates. Excellent calcium source.

description: Warm-season grass producing ears of sweet kernels. Wind-pollinated, plant in blocks.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - B1 (Thiamin), B9 (Folate), C, B5, B3. Minerals - Phosphorus, Manganese, Magnesium, Zinc, Iron, Potassium.
how_to_grow: Direct sow after soil 60F. Plant in blocks of 4+ rows for pollination. Space 10-12in, rows 30-36in. Full sun, rich soil. Side-dress nitrogen when knee-high.
insect_control: Corn earworm, European corn borer, raccoons. Mineral oil on silk tips. Bt for borers. Electric fence for raccoons.
tips: Harvest when silks brown, kernels milky when punctured. Sugar converts to starch quickly; eat immediately.

description: Warm-season vine producing crisp refreshing fruits. Bush varieties for small spaces.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - K, C, A, B5. Minerals - Potassium, Manganese, Magnesium, Phosphorus, Copper.
how_to_grow: Direct sow after last frost when soil warm. Plant along trellis 12in apart or hills 36in. Full sun, rich moist soil. Water consistently.
insect_control: Cucumber beetles spread bacterial wilt. Row covers until flowering. Hand pick. Powdery mildew common.
tips: Trellising saves space, straighter fruit. Pick young for best flavor. Overripe signals plant to stop producing.

description: Entirely edible plant: leaves, flowers, and roots. Highly nutritious.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - K, A, C, E, B6, B1, B2. Minerals - Iron, Calcium, Potassium, Manganese, Magnesium, Phosphorus, Copper.
how_to_grow: Direct sow spring or fall. Grows in almost any soil. Full sun to partial shade. Space 6-8in. Blanch by covering to reduce bitterness.
insect_control: Virtually pest-free. No significant problems.
tips: Harvest young leaves before flowering for mildest flavor. Roots roasted as coffee substitute. Flowers make wine.

description: Feathery annual herb used in pickling and fish dishes. Attracts beneficial insects.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - A, C, B9 (Folate). Minerals - Iron, Manganese, Calcium, Magnesium, Phosphorus, Potassium.
how_to_grow: Direct sow after last frost. Does not transplant well. Full sun, well-drained soil. Space 12in. May need staking. Self-seeds prolifically.
insect_control: Few problems. Attracts beneficial insects. Host plant for swallowtail butterflies.
tips: Harvest leaves anytime. Seed heads when brown. Fernleaf variety compact and slow to bolt. Plant near cucumbers.

description: Warm-season nightshade producing glossy purple, white, or striped fruits.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - B1, B6, K, C, B9, B3. Minerals - Manganese, Potassium, Copper, Magnesium, Phosphorus, Iron.
how_to_grow: Start indoors 8-10 weeks before last frost. Transplant when soil 70F+. Full sun, rich soil. Space 18-24in. Stake. Harvest when glossy and firm.
insect_control: Flea beetles primary pest. Row covers on young plants. Colorado potato beetle, aphids, spider mites.
tips: Harvest before seeds brown. Overripe becomes bitter. Japanese varieties produce more in short seasons. Black plastic mulch warms soil.

description: Slightly bitter salad green with curly or broad leaves. Blanching reduces bitterness.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - K, A, C, B9 (Folate). Minerals - Manganese, Potassium, Iron, Copper, Calcium.
how_to_grow: Direct sow spring or late summer. Cool weather preferred. Space 8-12in. Full sun to partial shade. Blanch by tying outer leaves 2-3 weeks before harvest.
insect_control: Aphids, slugs, snails. Beer traps. Insecticidal soap. Row covers.
tips: Fall crops less bitter. Escarole is broad-leaved endive. Belgian endive forced in darkness from roots.

description: Anise-flavored herb/vegetable. Florence fennel for bulb; herb fennel for fronds and seeds.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - C, B3, B9 (Folate), A. Minerals - Potassium, Manganese, Calcium, Iron, Magnesium, Phosphorus.
how_to_grow: Direct sow mid-summer for fall harvest. Full sun, well-drained soil. Space 6-12in. Mound soil around bulb. Keep watered. Harvest bulb at 3-4in.
insect_control: Few serious pests. Aphids occasionally. Host plant for beneficial insects.
tips: Do not plant near dill. Stress causes bolting. Fronds, bulb, and seeds all edible. Roasting sweetens bulb.

description: Deciduous tree producing sweet honey-flavored fruits. Thrives in Mediterranean climates.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - K, B6, B5, B1. Minerals - Potassium, Manganese, Magnesium, Calcium, Copper, Iron.
how_to_grow: Full sun against south-facing wall. Well-drained soil. Space 15-20ft. Prune to maintain size. Water during fruit development. Mulch heavily.
insect_control: Few serious pests. Fig rust in humid areas. Birds and wasps on ripe fruit. Use netting.
tips: Many varieties self-fertile. Fruit ripens on tree only. Harvest when drooping and soft. Excellent dried.

description: Pungent bulb essential in cuisines worldwide. Hardneck and softneck types.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - B6, C, B1. Minerals - Manganese, Selenium, Calcium, Phosphorus, Iron, Copper, Potassium.
how_to_grow: Plant cloves in fall, 4-6 weeks before ground freezes. Pointed end up, 2in deep, 6in apart. Full sun. Mulch heavily. Remove scapes on hardneck. Harvest when lower leaves brown.
insect_control: Few pest problems. Onion maggots occasionally. Good companion plant repelling many pests.
tips: Hardneck for cold climates, produces scapes. Softneck stores longer. Cure 2-3 weeks. Save largest cloves for replanting.

description: Tropical rhizome with spicy aromatic flavor used in cooking and medicine.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - B6, C, B3, B2. Minerals - Manganese, Magnesium, Potassium, Copper, Phosphorus, Iron.
how_to_grow: Plant rhizome pieces with growth buds in spring. Rich moist well-drained soil. Partial shade. Space 8-12in. Keep moist. Harvest after 8-10 months when leaves yellow.
insect_control: Few problems. Root rot from overwatering. Occasionally aphids. Good drainage prevents issues.
tips: Buy organic ginger from grocery for planting. Grows well in containers indoors. Young ginger milder and more tender.

description: Large thistle-like perennial producing edible flower buds. Dramatic ornamental.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - K, C, B9 (Folate), B6. Minerals - Magnesium, Manganese, Potassium, Phosphorus, Iron, Copper, Calcium.
how_to_grow: Start from divisions or seed. Full sun, rich well-drained soil. Space 4-6ft. Heavy feeder. Water deeply. Harvest buds before scales open.
insect_control: Aphids on buds. Blast with water or insecticidal soap. Slugs on young plants. Plume moth larvae.
tips: Perennial zones 7-11. Grow as annual in cold by vernalizing. Cut back after harvest for fall crop. Productive 4-5 years.

description: Subtropical citrus tree producing large tart-sweet fruits.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - C, A, B1, B5, B9. Minerals - Potassium, Copper, Magnesium, Manganese, Phosphorus.
how_to_grow: Full sun, well-drained soil. Space 15-20ft. Water deeply and regularly. Citrus fertilizer 3x/year. Protect from frost.
insect_control: Citrus leafminer, scale, mites. Horticultural oil. Encourage ladybugs. Monitor for citrus greening.
tips: Fruit improves on tree. Trees bear at 3-4 years. Ruby red sweeter than white. Stores well on tree.

description: Deciduous vine producing clusters of sweet fruits for fresh eating, wine, juice, raisins.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - K, C, B1, B6. Minerals - Copper, Potassium, Manganese, Iron, Phosphorus.
how_to_grow: Full sun with good air circulation. Well-drained soil. Space 6-8ft on trellis. Prune heavily late winter. Water deeply but infrequently.
insect_control: Japanese beetles, grape berry moth, powdery mildew. Pheromone traps. Sulfur spray. Good air circulation.
tips: Choose varieties for your climate. Prune 90% of previous growth. Harvest when sweet and easily detached.

description: Tropical fruit tree producing aromatic fruits extremely rich in vitamin C.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - C (extremely high), A, B9, B3, B6. Minerals - Potassium, Manganese, Copper, Magnesium, Phosphorus.
how_to_grow: Full sun, well-drained soil. Space 10-15ft. Water regularly during fruit development. Fertilize quarterly. Protect from frost.
insect_control: Fruit flies main pest. Use traps and bag fruit. Scale and mealybugs with horticultural oil.
tips: Ripe when skin turns green to yellow. 4x vitamin C of oranges. Strawberry guava hardier alternative.

description: Vigorous perennial grown for pungent root used as condiment. Extremely cold hardy.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - C, B9, B6. Minerals - Calcium, Potassium, Manganese, Magnesium, Zinc, Iron, Phosphorus.
how_to_grow: Plant root cuttings early spring at 45 degrees. Full sun to partial shade. Rich moist soil. Space 18in. Harvest roots in fall after frost.
insect_control: Flea beetles occasionally. Generally pest-free due to pungent compounds.
tips: Contain with buried barrier as it spreads aggressively. Grate fresh immediately. Mix with vinegar to preserve heat.

description: Extremely nutritious leafy green thriving in cool weather. Many varieties.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - K (extremely high), A, C, B6, B9, B2, E. Minerals - Manganese, Copper, Calcium, Potassium, Iron, Magnesium.
how_to_grow: Direct sow or transplant early spring or late summer. Full sun to partial shade. Space 12-18in. Rich moist soil. Harvest outer leaves. Extremely cold hardy.
insect_control: Cabbage worms, aphids, flea beetles. Bt, row covers, neem oil.
tips: One of most nutrient-dense foods. Massage raw leaves for salads. Dehydrate for chips. Overwinters in many climates.

description: Vigorous deciduous vine producing fuzzy brown fruits with bright green flesh.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - C (very high), K, E, B9. Minerals - Potassium, Copper, Manganese, Magnesium, Phosphorus.
how_to_grow: Full sun with wind protection. Rich well-drained soil. Need male and female plants. Strong trellis. Space 10-15ft. Prune heavily winter.
insect_control: Few serious pests. Root rot in poor drainage. Japanese beetles occasionally.
tips: Hardy kiwi (A. arguta) survives -25F with smooth skin. Fruit ripens off vine. Vines produce at 3-5 years.

description: Aromatic perennial herb with purple flowers. Drought tolerant.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - A, C. Minerals - Calcium, Iron, Magnesium, Potassium.
how_to_grow: Full sun in well-drained alkaline soil. Space 12-18in. Do not overwater. Prune after flowering. Avoid clay or wet soils. Mulch with gravel.
insect_control: Very few pests. Deer and rabbit resistant. Repels moths, fleas, mosquitoes. Root rot from overwatering.
tips: Harvest just as buds open for strongest fragrance. Dry hanging upside down. English lavender hardiest. Excellent bee plant.

description: Mild-flavored allium with thick white stalk. Hardy and versatile.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - K, A, C, B6, B9. Minerals - Manganese, Iron, Copper, Magnesium, Calcium, Potassium.
how_to_grow: Start indoors 10-12 weeks before last frost. Transplant into 6in deep trenches. Hill soil around stems. Full sun, rich moist soil. Space 6in.
insect_control: Onion maggots, leek moth. Row covers. Rotate crops. Generally pest-resistant.
tips: Very cold hardy; overwinter in ground with mulch. Longer white portion = more usable leek. Wash thoroughly.

description: Tropical grass with intense lemon flavor used in Asian cuisine.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - A, C, B9. Minerals - Potassium, Manganese, Iron, Magnesium, Zinc, Copper.
how_to_grow: Plant divisions or grocery store stalks in full sun after last frost. Rich moist soil. Space 24in. Water regularly. Grows 3-5ft. Bring indoors before frost.
insect_control: Virtually pest-free. Citronella compounds repel mosquitoes.
tips: Root grocery store stalks in water. Harvest outer stalks at 12+ inches. Use lower 4-6in. Makes excellent tea.

description: Subtropical citrus trees producing tart fruits essential in cooking.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - C (very high), B6, B9, A. Minerals - Potassium, Calcium, Iron, Magnesium, Phosphorus.
how_to_grow: Full sun, well-drained soil. Protect from frost. Water deeply. Citrus fertilizer 3-4x/year. Excellent container plants.
insect_control: Scale, aphids, citrus leafminer, spider mites. Horticultural oil. Insecticidal soap.
tips: Meyer lemons hardier and sweeter. Key limes more cold-sensitive. Fruit stays on tree months. Indoor trees need bright south window.

description: Cool-season salad green in many types: loose-leaf, romaine, butterhead, crisphead.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - K, A, C, B9 (Folate). Minerals - Manganese, Iron, Potassium, Calcium, Magnesium.
how_to_grow: Direct sow or transplant early spring or fall. Bolts in heat. Space 6-12in. Partial shade in summer. Keep moist. Harvest outer leaves or whole head.
insect_control: Aphids, slugs, rabbits. Row covers, beer traps, fencing.
tips: Succession sow every 2 weeks. Heat-tolerant varieties for summer. Cut-and-come-again extends production.

description: Perennial legume with sweet-flavored roots used in confections and herbal medicine.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - B1, B2, B3, B5, E. Minerals - Phosphorus, Calcium, Iron, Magnesium, Potassium, Zinc.
how_to_grow: Plant root divisions in spring. Full sun, deep well-drained soil. Space 18in. Water moderately. Harvest roots after 3-4 years.
insect_control: Few pest problems. Spider mites occasionally in dry conditions.
tips: Roots 50x sweeter than sugar. Harvest in fall, dry and store. Nitrogen-fixing legume improves soil.

description: Evergreen tree producing clusters of sweet-tart orange fruits in late winter/early spring.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - A, C, B6, B9. Minerals - Potassium, Manganese, Copper, Iron, Calcium.
how_to_grow: Full sun to partial shade, well-drained soil. Space 15-20ft. Water regularly when young. Thin fruit clusters. Protect flowers from hard freezes.
insect_control: Few serious pests. Fire blight occasionally. Fruit flies on ripe fruit.
tips: Fruit ripens late winter when little else available. Seeds toxic. Trees ornamental year-round.

description: Sweet aromatic herb related to oregano but milder. Essential in French cuisine.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - K, A, C, B9. Minerals - Iron, Calcium, Manganese, Magnesium, Potassium, Zinc.
how_to_grow: Start indoors 6-8 weeks before last frost. Full sun, well-drained soil. Space 8-10in. Pinch tips for bushiness. Harvest before flowering.
insect_control: Few problems. Aphids occasionally. Spider mites in dry conditions.
tips: More delicate than oregano; add at end of cooking. Dry by hanging bundles. Excellent container plant.

description: Tropical evergreen tree producing large sweet aromatic fruits.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - C, A, B6, E, K, B9. Minerals - Copper, Potassium, Magnesium, Manganese, Phosphorus.
how_to_grow: Full sun, well-drained soil. Space 25-30ft. Water regularly when young. Fertilize 3-4x/year. Prune to control size. Protect from frost.
insect_control: Mango seed weevil, fruit flies, anthracnose. Bag fruit. Copper spray for anthracnose.
tips: Grafted trees fruit in 3-4 years. Dwarf varieties for containers. Fruit ripens off tree.

description: Warm-season vines producing sweet aromatic fruits. Need long hot growing season.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - C, A, B6, B9, K. Minerals - Potassium, Copper, Magnesium, Manganese, Iron.
how_to_grow: Direct sow after soil 70F or start indoors 3-4 weeks early. Hills 4-6ft apart. Full sun, rich soil. Consistent water until sizing, then reduce.
insect_control: Cucumber beetles, aphids, powdery mildew. Row covers until flowering. Neem oil. Good air circulation.
tips: Cantaloupe ripe when stem slips easily. Honeydew when blossom end gives slightly. Cardboard under fruit prevents rot.

description: Vigorous spreading perennial herb with cooling menthol flavor.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - A, C, B9, B2. Minerals - Iron, Manganese, Copper, Magnesium, Calcium, Potassium.
how_to_grow: Plant divisions in spring. Full sun to partial shade. Moist rich soil. ALWAYS plant in containers or with barriers as it spreads aggressively.
insect_control: Few problems. Mint rust occasionally. Spider mites in dry conditions. Strong scent deters pests.
tips: Grow in containers to prevent spreading. Harvest before flowering. Many varieties: chocolate, apple, orange.

description: Fast-growing green with spicy flavor. Leaves raw or cooked; seeds as condiment.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - K, A, C, E, B9, B6. Minerals - Manganese, Calcium, Potassium, Iron, Magnesium.
how_to_grow: Direct sow early spring or fall. Bolts in heat. Space 6-8in. Full sun to partial shade. Keep moist. Harvest young leaves. Matures 30-40 days.
insect_control: Flea beetles, aphids, cabbage worms. Row covers, neem oil. Fast growth outpaces damage.
tips: Succession plant every 2-3 weeks. Fall crops less pungent. Let some go to seed for homemade mustard.

description: Smooth-skinned peach variant with slightly firmer flesh.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - C, A, E, K, B3, B6. Minerals - Potassium, Copper, Phosphorus, Manganese, Iron.
how_to_grow: Full sun, well-drained soil. Space 15-20ft. Prune to open center. Thin fruit to 6in apart. Water deeply during fruit development.
insect_control: Brown rot, peach leaf curl, oriental fruit moth. Copper spray fall. Dormant oil. Bag fruit.
tips: Most self-fertile. Harvest when gives to pressure. Dwarf varieties for small spaces. Chill hours important.

description: Heat-loving plant producing edible seed pods. Beautiful hibiscus-like flowers.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - K, C, A, B9, B1, B6. Minerals - Manganese, Magnesium, Potassium, Calcium, Iron.
how_to_grow: Direct sow after soil 65F. Soak seeds overnight. Full sun. Space 12-18in. Plants grow 4-6ft. Harvest pods at 2-3in every 1-2 days.
insect_control: Aphids, corn earworm, stink bugs. Neem oil, insecticidal soap. Hand pick larger pests.
tips: Wear gloves (irritating spines). Clemson Spineless is smooth variety. Roasting reduces sliminess.

description: Evergreen tree producing fruits for oil and table use. Extremely long-lived and drought tolerant.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - E, A, K. Minerals - Iron, Copper, Calcium, Sodium, Potassium.
how_to_grow: Full sun, well-drained soil. Extremely drought tolerant. Space 20-30ft. Minimal pruning. Needs winter chill but damaged by hard freezes.
insect_control: Olive fruit fly main pest. Traps and kaolin clay. Scale with horticultural oil.
tips: Trees live centuries. Most need cross-pollination. Fruit must be cured before eating (raw very bitter).

description: Essential kitchen staple. Day-length sensitive with many types.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - C, B6, B9, B1. Minerals - Manganese, Potassium, Phosphorus, Copper, Magnesium.
how_to_grow: Plant sets, transplants, or seeds early spring. Choose day-length variety for latitude. Full sun, loose fertile soil. Space 4-6in. Cure when tops fall.
insect_control: Onion maggots, thrips, downy mildew. Row covers. Rotate crops. Companion plant with carrots.
tips: Short-day for south, long-day for north. Cure thoroughly for storage. Sets easiest for beginners.

description: Subtropical evergreen citrus trees producing sweet juicy fruits.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - C (very high), A, B1, B9, B5. Minerals - Potassium, Calcium, Magnesium, Copper.
how_to_grow: Full sun, well-drained soil. Space 12-20ft. Water deeply. Citrus fertilizer 3-4x/year. Protect from frost. Minimal pruning.
insect_control: Scale, aphids, citrus leafminer, whitefly. Horticultural oil. Monitor for citrus greening.
tips: Color not always ripeness indicator in warm climates. Taste test. Tangerines easier to peel. Dwarf for containers.

description: Robust perennial herb essential in Italian and Greek cuisine. Stronger dried.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - K, E, C, A, B6. Minerals - Iron, Manganese, Calcium, Magnesium, Potassium, Copper.
how_to_grow: Plant divisions or seed in spring. Full sun, well-drained soil. Space 12in. Drought tolerant. Cut back after flowering. Divide every 3-4 years.
insect_control: Virtually pest-free. Strong oils deter insects. Good companion plant.
tips: Greek oregano strongest flavor. Harvest before flowers open. Flavor stronger dried than fresh. Spreads readily.

description: Biennial herb with flat-leaf and curly varieties. Extremely nutritious.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - K (extremely high), C, A, B9. Minerals - Iron, Potassium, Calcium, Magnesium, Manganese.
how_to_grow: Start indoors 8-10 weeks before last frost (slow germinator). Soak seeds overnight. Full sun to partial shade. Space 6-8in. Keep moist.
insect_control: Parsleyworm (swallowtail caterpillar) main pest. Hand pick or relocate.
tips: Flat-leaf stronger flavor. Biennial: leaves first year, flowers second. Freeze in ice cube trays.

description: Sweet root vegetable that improves after frost. Long growing season required.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - C, K, B9, E. Minerals - Manganese, Potassium, Magnesium, Phosphorus, Zinc, Iron.
how_to_grow: Direct sow fresh seed early spring. Germination slow (2-4 weeks). Deep loose soil. Space 3-4in. Full sun. Keep moist. Harvest after first frost.
insect_control: Carrot rust fly, parsnip canker. Row covers. Rotate crops. Resistant varieties.
tips: Always use fresh seed (loses viability after one year). Frost converts starch to sugar. Can overwinter in ground.

description: Deciduous fruit tree producing juicy sweet stone fruits.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - C, A, E, K, B3, B6. Minerals - Potassium, Copper, Manganese, Phosphorus, Magnesium.
how_to_grow: Full sun, well-drained soil. Space 15-20ft. Prune to open center. Thin fruit 6-8in apart. Most self-fertile.
insect_control: Peach leaf curl (copper in fall), brown rot, oriental fruit moth. Dormant oil. Good sanitation.
tips: Choose appropriate chill hours. Harvest when gives to pressure. Tree-ripened far superior. Short-lived trees (15-20 years).

description: Deciduous fruit tree producing sweet buttery fruits. Easier organic than apples.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - C, K, B9, B6. Minerals - Copper, Potassium, Manganese, Magnesium, Phosphorus.
how_to_grow: Full sun, well-drained soil. Most need cross-pollination. Space 15-20ft. Prune to central leader. Thin fruit 6in apart.
insect_control: Fire blight most serious (prune 12in below damage). Codling moth, pear psylla. Dormant oil. Kaolin clay.
tips: Harvest mature but firm; ripen off tree. European ripen inside out. Asian ripen on tree. Fire blight resistant varieties recommended.

description: Cool-season legume: shelling, snap, and snow types. Fixes nitrogen.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - K, C, B1, B9, B6, B3. Minerals - Manganese, Phosphorus, Iron, Zinc, Magnesium, Potassium.
how_to_grow: Direct sow as early as soil workable. Inoculate with rhizobium. Trellis for climbers. Space 2-3in. Full sun. Keep moist. Harvest frequently.
insect_control: Pea aphids, powdery mildew, pea weevil. Insecticidal soap. Good air circulation. Rotate crops.
tips: Sugar snap most versatile. Succession plant every 2 weeks. Also plant in fall. Stop producing in heat.

description: Warm-season fruits from sweet bells to fiery hot varieties.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - C (extremely high), A, B6, K, E, B9. Minerals - Potassium, Manganese, Magnesium, Iron, Copper.
how_to_grow: Start indoors 8-10 weeks before last frost. Transplant when soil 65F+. Full sun. Space 18-24in. Stake tall varieties. Consistent water.
insect_control: Aphids, pepper maggots, hornworms. Row covers early. Hand pick hornworms. Consistent watering prevents blossom end rot.
tips: Colored peppers are fully ripe green peppers with more vitamins. Hot peppers hotter with stress. Wear gloves for hot varieties.

description: Deciduous tree producing sweet orange fruits in fall.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - A, C, E, K, B6. Minerals - Manganese, Potassium, Copper, Phosphorus, Magnesium.
how_to_grow: Full sun, well-drained soil. Space 15-20ft. Minimal pruning. Water regularly when young. Most Asian varieties self-fertile.
insect_control: Few serious pests. Persimmon psylla and mealybugs occasionally. Very low maintenance.
tips: Astringent types must be fully soft. Non-astringent (Fuyu) eaten firm like apple. Excellent dried.

description: Deciduous tree producing green nuts. Requires hot dry summers and cold winters.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - B6, B1, E, K. Minerals - Copper, Manganese, Phosphorus, Magnesium, Iron, Potassium.
how_to_grow: Full sun, well-drained soil. Need male and female trees. Space 20ft. Extremely drought tolerant. Hot summers, cold winters required.
insect_control: Few pests in dry climates. Navel orangeworm in humid areas. Verticillium wilt possible.
tips: Trees take 5-7 years to bear. Harvest when hulls separate. Dry immediately. Shells split naturally when ripe.

description: Deciduous fruit tree producing sweet or tart stone fruits.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - C, K, A, B6. Minerals - Potassium, Copper, Manganese, Magnesium, Phosphorus.
how_to_grow: Full sun, well-drained soil. Japanese need cross-pollination; some European self-fertile. Space 15-20ft. Thin fruit 4-6in apart.
insect_control: Plum curculio, brown rot, black knot. Dormant oil, copper spray. Remove infected branches. Good sanitation.
tips: Japanese ripen earlier, eat fresh. European better for drying (prunes). Harvest when gives to pressure.

description: Deciduous shrub/tree producing fruits filled with jewel-like arils. Drought tolerant.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - C, K, B9, E. Minerals - Potassium, Copper, Manganese, Phosphorus, Magnesium.
how_to_grow: Full sun, well-drained soil. Space 12-15ft. Drought tolerant. Prune to 3-5 trunks. Water deeply but infrequently.
insect_control: Few pests. Leaf-footed bugs occasionally. Fruit rot in humid climates. Good air circulation.
tips: Ripe when skin deepens and sounds metallic when tapped. Arils stain. Trees live 200+ years.

description: Starchy tuber, worlds fourth-largest food crop. Easy to grow with high yields.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - C, B6, B3, B9, B5, B1. Minerals - Potassium, Manganese, Magnesium, Phosphorus, Iron, Copper.
how_to_grow: Plant certified seed potatoes 4in deep, 12in apart. Hill soil around stems as they grow. Full sun, loose acidic soil. Water consistently.
insect_control: Colorado potato beetle (hand pick, Bt), late blight, scab. Rotate crops. Certified disease-free seed.
tips: Green skin toxic (solanine); keep covered. Cure 2 weeks before storing. Grow in bags, towers, or raised beds.

description: Warm-season vine producing large fruits for cooking, carving, and seeds.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - A (very high), C, E, B2, B6. Minerals - Potassium, Copper, Manganese, Iron, Phosphorus.
how_to_grow: Direct sow after last frost in hills 6-8ft apart. Full sun, rich soil. Heavy feeder. Water at base. Limit 2-3 fruits per vine for size.
insect_control: Squash vine borer, squash bugs, powdery mildew. Row covers until flowering. Hand pick bugs. Bt for borers.
tips: Cardboard under fruit prevents rot. Cure in sun 10 days. Seeds nutritious roasted. Pie pumpkins differ from carving types.

description: Deciduous tree producing aromatic golden fruits for preserves. Rarely eaten raw.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - C, B6, B1, B2. Minerals - Copper, Iron, Potassium, Magnesium, Phosphorus.
how_to_grow: Full sun, well-drained soil. Space 12-15ft. Self-fertile. Minimal pruning. Very adaptable. Hardy and low maintenance.
insect_control: Fire blight, codling moth, quince leaf blight. Prune infected branches. Dormant oil.
tips: Must be cooked (too hard raw). Turns pink when cooked. Excellent jelly and paste (membrillo). Extremely fragrant.

description: Italian chicory with red and white leaves. Slightly bitter, mellows when grilled.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - K, C, B9, E. Minerals - Copper, Manganese, Potassium, Iron, Zinc.
how_to_grow: Direct sow mid-summer for fall harvest. Needs cool temps for heads. Space 8-12in. Full sun to partial shade. Keep moist.
insect_control: Aphids, slugs. Generally pest-resistant due to bitter compounds. Row covers. Beer traps.
tips: Color intensifies with cold. Cut heads, leave roots to resprout. Grilling reduces bitterness.

description: Fastest-growing root vegetable, ready in 3-4 weeks.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - C, B9, B6. Minerals - Potassium, Manganese, Calcium, Iron, Magnesium.
how_to_grow: Direct sow as soon as soil workable. Succession plant every 1-2 weeks. Space 1-2in. Full sun to partial shade. Keep moist.
insect_control: Flea beetles, root maggots. Row covers. Fast growth outpaces damage. Good trap crop.
tips: Bolts in heat. Daikon needs longer season. Seed pods also edible. Interplant with slow crops as markers.

description: Bramble producing delicate sweet berries. Red, black, and golden types.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - C, K, E, B9. Minerals - Manganese, Magnesium, Copper, Iron, Potassium.
how_to_grow: Full sun, well-drained slightly acidic soil. Space 2-3ft, rows 6-8ft. Trellis support. Prune spent floricanes. Mulch heavily.
insect_control: Spotted wing drosophila, cane borer, spider mites. Bird netting. Prune infected canes. Dormant spray.
tips: Summer-bearing on second-year canes. Everbearing on first-year (mow all in winter). Harvest every 1-2 days. Freeze well.

description: Perennial vegetable with tart edible stalks. Leaves are toxic.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - K, C, A. Minerals - Calcium, Manganese, Potassium, Magnesium, Iron.
how_to_grow: Plant crowns early spring. Full sun to partial shade. Rich moist well-drained soil. Space 3-4ft. Do not harvest first year. Remove flower stalks.
insect_control: Few problems. Crown rot in poor drainage. Very low maintenance.
tips: Only eat stalks; leaves contain toxic oxalic acid. Pull stalks, dont cut. Red varieties sweeter. Stop harvesting mid-summer.

description: Evergreen aromatic shrub with needle-like leaves. Drought tolerant.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - A, C, B6, B9. Minerals - Iron, Calcium, Manganese, Magnesium, Copper.
how_to_grow: Full sun, well-drained soil. Space 2-3ft. Drought tolerant. Do not overwater. Prune after flowering. Excellent container plant.
insect_control: Very few pests. Powdery mildew in humid conditions. Root rot from overwatering.
tips: Harvest sprigs anytime. Woody stems make skewers. Prostrate varieties as ground cover. Flowers attract bees.

description: Root vegetable cross between turnip and cabbage. Sweet nutty flavor after frost.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - C, B6, B1, B9. Minerals - Potassium, Manganese, Magnesium, Phosphorus, Calcium.
how_to_grow: Direct sow mid-summer for fall harvest (90 days). Space 6-8in. Full sun, loose soil. Keep moist. Harvest after first frost at 3-5in diameter.
insect_control: Flea beetles, cabbage root maggot, aphids. Row covers. Rotate from brassicas.
tips: Flavor sweetens after frost. Stores months in root cellar. Mash, roast, or add to stews. Greens also edible.

description: Aromatic perennial herb with velvety gray-green leaves. Essential in poultry seasoning.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - K, A, C, B6, B9, E. Minerals - Iron, Calcium, Manganese, Magnesium, Zinc.
how_to_grow: Full sun, well-drained soil. Space 18-24in. Drought tolerant. Prune in spring. Replace every 4-5 years as plants become woody.
insect_control: Few problems. Slugs on young plants. Root rot from overwatering. Generally pest-resistant.
tips: Harvest before flowering for best flavor. Dry by hanging. Pineapple sage has fruity scent. Excellent bee plant.

description: Mild-flavored green onions harvested young. Quick-growing and versatile.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - K, C, A, B9. Minerals - Iron, Calcium, Manganese, Potassium, Copper.
how_to_grow: Direct sow as soon as soil workable. Succession plant every 2-3 weeks. Space 1in. Full sun to partial shade. Keep moist. Harvest when pencil-thick.
insect_control: Onion maggots occasionally. Generally pest-free. Good companion plant.
tips: Regrow from root ends in water. Perennial bunching onions overwinter and multiply.

description: Mild sweet onion relative that multiplies into clusters. Prized in French cuisine.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - B6, C, A, B9. Minerals - Manganese, Potassium, Iron, Copper, Phosphorus.
how_to_grow: Plant sets fall or early spring, 1in deep, 6in apart. Full sun, well-drained soil. Each set multiplies into 4-12 bulbs. Harvest when tops yellow.
insect_control: Same as onions. Row covers. Rotate crops. Generally fewer problems.
tips: Plant largest bulbs for biggest harvest. Save smallest for replanting. Milder than onions. Store 6+ months.

description: Nutrient-dense cool-season green. Bolts quickly in heat.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - K (extremely high), A, C, B9, B2, B6, E. Minerals - Manganese, Magnesium, Iron, Potassium, Calcium, Copper.
how_to_grow: Direct sow early spring or fall. Bolts in heat and long days. Space 3-4in. Partial shade in warm weather. Keep moist. Matures 35-45 days.
insect_control: Leaf miners, aphids, downy mildew. Row covers. Remove mined leaves. Good air circulation.
tips: Fall crops better (less bolting). Baby spinach ready in 3 weeks. High in oxalates; cooking reduces them.

description: Prolific warm-season vegetable including zucchini, yellow squash, pattypan.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - C, A, B6, K, B9, B2. Minerals - Manganese, Potassium, Magnesium, Phosphorus, Copper.
how_to_grow: Direct sow after last frost in hills 3-4ft apart. Full sun, rich soil. Water deeply at base. Harvest at 6-8in. Check daily.
insect_control: Squash vine borer, squash bugs, powdery mildew. Row covers until flowering. Hand pick bugs. Succession plant.
tips: One plant produces abundantly. Harvest frequently. Male flowers first; female have tiny fruit at base. Flowers edible.

description: Long-season vine producing hard-shelled fruits that store for months.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - A (very high), C, B6, E, K, B1, B9. Minerals - Potassium, Manganese, Magnesium, Copper, Iron.
how_to_grow: Direct sow after last frost in hills 6-8ft apart. Full sun, rich soil. Long season (80-120 days). Harvest when skin hard and cant be dented.
insect_control: Same as summer squash. Moschata types (butternut) vine borer resistant. Row covers. Good air circulation.
tips: Cure in sun 10 days for storage. Store at 50-55F for 3-6 months. Butternut and delicata store best. Seeds nutritious roasted.

description: Low-growing perennial producing sweet red berries. June-bearing, everbearing, day-neutral types.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - C (very high), B9, K, B5, B6. Minerals - Manganese, Potassium, Magnesium, Copper, Iron.
how_to_grow: Plant crowns early spring with crown at soil level. Full sun, well-drained slightly acidic soil. Space 12-18in. Mulch with straw. Remove runners first year.
insect_control: Slugs, birds, gray mold (botrytis). Straw mulch keeps fruit clean. Bird netting. Good air circulation.
tips: June-bearing: one large crop. Day-neutral: continuous. Replace every 3-4 years. Alpine strawberries intensely flavored.

description: Tall annual producing edible seeds and oil. Cheerful flowers attract pollinators.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - E (very high), B1, B6, B9, B3. Minerals - Selenium, Copper, Manganese, Magnesium, Phosphorus, Iron.
how_to_grow: Direct sow after last frost. Space 6-12in. Full sun. Stake tall varieties. Water deeply. Harvest when back of head brown and seeds plump.
insect_control: Birds main threat. Cover heads with mesh. Sunflower moth larvae. Aphids on stems. Generally robust.
tips: Confection types for eating; oil types for pressing. Dry heads upside down in bag. Shells make good mulch.

description: Colorful leafy green related to beets. Heat tolerant. Rainbow-colored stalks.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - K (extremely high), A, C, E, B6, B2, B9. Minerals - Magnesium, Manganese, Iron, Potassium, Copper, Calcium.
how_to_grow: Direct sow or transplant spring. Tolerates heat better than spinach. Space 8-12in. Full sun to partial shade. Keep moist. Harvest outer leaves.
insect_control: Leaf miners main pest. Remove affected leaves. Row covers. Slugs in wet conditions.
tips: Cut-and-come-again extends season spring through fall. Both leaves and stalks edible. Tolerates light frost.

description: Perennial herb with anise-like flavor essential in French cuisine.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - A, C, B6, B9. Minerals - Iron, Manganese, Calcium, Magnesium, Potassium.
how_to_grow: Plant divisions or cuttings (French tarragon not from seed). Full sun, well-drained soil. Space 18-24in. Drought tolerant. Divide every 3-4 years.
insect_control: Virtually pest-free. Root rot in wet soil main issue.
tips: French tarragon best flavor (vegetative propagation only). Russian (from seed) inferior. Preserve in vinegar or freeze.

description: Low-growing perennial herb with tiny aromatic leaves. Many varieties.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - K, C, A, B6, B9. Minerals - Iron, Manganese, Calcium, Magnesium, Copper.
how_to_grow: Full sun, well-drained soil. Space 6-12in. Drought tolerant. Trim after flowering. Replace every 3-4 years.
insect_control: Virtually pest-free. Spider mites in very dry conditions. Root rot from overwatering.
tips: Flavor strongest before flowering. Lemon thyme excellent with fish. Creeping varieties as ground cover. Attracts bees.

description: Most popular home garden vegetable. Thousands of varieties from cherry to beefsteak.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - C, A, K, B1, B6, B9, B3, B2, B5, E. Minerals - Molybdenum, Potassium, Manganese, Chromium, Copper, Magnesium, Iron, Phosphorus.
how_to_grow: Full sun, amend soil with compost. pH 6. Start indoors 6 weeks before last frost. Space 2ft+. Cage or stake. Pinch tops after 3-4 fruit groups. Heavy feeders. Keep moist not wet.
insect_control: Tomato hornworm: Bt or hand remove. Botanical insecticide-fungicide for blight, leaf spot, wilt.
tips: Pick fully ripe for best nutrition (4x more beta-carotene than green). Determinate fruit all at once; indeterminate all season.

description: Fast-growing root vegetable with edible greens.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - C, K, B9, B6. Minerals - Manganese, Potassium, Calcium, Magnesium, Phosphorus.
how_to_grow: Direct sow early spring or late summer. Fast (30-60 days). Space 3-4in. Full sun, loose soil. Keep moist. Harvest at 2-3in diameter.
insect_control: Flea beetles, cabbage root maggot, aphids. Row covers. Fast growth outpaces damage.
tips: Baby turnips sweetest. Fall crops sweeter (frost improves). Hakurei exceptionally mild. Greens more nutritious than roots.

description: Large deciduous tree producing rich flavorful nuts.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - B6, B9, B1, E. Minerals - Manganese, Copper, Phosphorus, Magnesium, Iron, Zinc.
how_to_grow: Full sun, deep well-drained soil. Space 40-60ft. Need cross-pollination. Deep taproot. Water deeply when young.
insect_control: Walnut husk fly, codling moth, walnut blight. Traps. Copper spray. Low maintenance once established.
tips: Trees produce juglone inhibiting nearby plants. Harvest when hulls split. Remove hulls promptly (stain). Bear at 4-7 years grafted.

description: Peppery aquatic green extremely high in nutrients. Grows in shallow water or very moist soil.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - K (very high), A, C, E, B6, B2, B1. Minerals - Calcium, Manganese, Potassium, Phosphorus, Magnesium, Iron.
how_to_grow: Grow in shallow running water, containers with saucers kept full, or very moist soil. Partial shade. Cool temps. Root cuttings in water.
insect_control: Few problems. Snails and slugs. Clean water source important.
tips: Grow from grocery store watercress. Extremely nutrient-dense. Peppery flavor mellows with cooking.

description: Large warm-season vine producing sweet juicy fruits. Needs long hot season and space.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - C, A, B6, B1, B5. Minerals - Potassium, Magnesium, Copper, Manganese, Phosphorus.
how_to_grow: Direct sow after soil 70F or start indoors 3-4 weeks early. Hills 6-8ft apart. Full sun, sandy soil. Black plastic mulch. Water until sizing then reduce.
insect_control: Cucumber beetles, aphids, fusarium wilt. Row covers until flowering. Resistant varieties. Neem oil.
tips: Thump test: ripe sounds hollow. Ground spot turns creamy yellow. Icebox varieties for small gardens. Seeds nutritious roasted.

description: Young grass of wheat plant juiced for concentrated nutrients. Easy indoor growing.
vitamin_mineral_content: Vitamins - A, C, E, K, B1, B2, B3, B6, B9. Minerals - Iron, Calcium, Magnesium, Manganese, Zinc, Potassium, Selenium.
how_to_grow: Soak wheat berries overnight. Spread on tray with thin soil. Mist daily. Indirect light. Harvest at 6-7in (7-10 days). Compost and restart.
insect_control: Mold main issue. Good air circulation. Do not overwater. Fresh soil each batch.
tips: Juice immediately after cutting. Grow in succession for daily supply. Cannot digest without juicing. Start with small amounts.
